mechanism_narrative: Declining nonfarm payrolls signal worsening labor market conditions, which depresses long-term yield expectations through reduced growth and inflation outlook.
failure_condition: If 20-day yield increases more than 10bp while PAYEMS is declining over the same period (rolling 3-month), reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls to or below 1%, it marks the effective zero lower bound. Historical precedent shows that long-term yields then rise as markets anticipate eventual inflation from policy accommodation.
failure_condition: If 10yr yield declines more than 5bp on average in instances where FEDFUNDS<=1% over 20-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high unemployment and near-zero interest rates indicates severe economic slack and extreme monetary accommodation. This joint condition historically leads to steepening yield curves as markets price in future recovery and inflation, driving the 10-year yield upward.
failure_condition: If in rolling 2-year periods with trigger active, the 10yr yield 20-day return is negative more than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when deflation is the primary macroeconomic fear (e.g., late 2008), any sign of rising inflation (CPI month-over-month increase above 0.1%) is an unexpected positive shock that triggers a sharp repricing of long-term yields upward as deflation risk diminishes.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL monthly increase > 0.1% is followed by a decline in 10yr yield of more than 10bp over the next 60 trading days in this regime, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production is a coincident indicator of economic activity. During recession, falling industrial production reduces demand for capital and depresses real yields, leading to lower nominal yields.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by more than 1% monthly but 10yr yield rises over the next 20 days, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A large positive surprise in retail sales during a recession signals a potential consumer-led recovery, causing a spike in long-term yields as growth expectations improve. The short horizon (5 days) captures the immediate market reaction.
failure_condition: If on days following such a surprise, the 10yr yield drops more than 5bp within 5 trading days, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in housing starts and industrial production deepens recessionary fears. This joint signal amplifies the flight-to-quality bid in Treasuries, pushing yields down more than either series alone would predict.
failure_condition: If in triggered 20-day windows the 10yr yield increases in more than 40% of cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-Lehman financial crisis regime, further declines in housing starts are especially toxic because they signal deepening mortgage market distress and credit contraction, which forces a flight to safe government bonds and lowers yields.
failure_condition: If after a 5% monthly drop in HOUST, the 10yr yield rises or stays flat over the next 60 trading days in the crisis regime, reject.
